commands.review_summary:
The PR normalizes impossible Apple-epoch imports to zero in `appleNullTime` and adds a focused unit test for sentinel, valid, and invalid timestamp inputs.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main imports positive `ZLASTMESSAGEDATE` values through `appleTime`, stores them as Unix timestamps, and later JSON-encodes `time.Time` fields for `status`, `chats`, and `unread`. I did not run a live archive reproduction because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor real-behavior proof plus normal maintainer review, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(whatsappdb): clamp impossible Apple-epoch timestamps so --json survives 0@status
target.body:
## Problem

`wacrawl --json chats` / `unread` / `status` fail with:

```
json: error calling MarshalJSON for type time.Time: Time.MarshalJSON: year outside of range [0,9999]
```

and emit empty stdout + exit 1. The human (table) output is unaffected, so it only surfaces under `--json`.

## Cause

WhatsApp Desktop stores a sentinel `ZLASTMESSAGEDATE` for pseudo-chats such as the `0@status` broadcast chat. `appleTime()` interprets it as a literal Apple-epoch value, which converts to an impossible year (~11001). That value lands in the archive, and `encoding/json` then can't marshal it (`time.Time.MarshalJSON` rejects years outside `[0,9999]`) for any command whose JSON output includes the row — `chats`/`unread` directly, `status` via `max(ts)`. It also makes the pseudo-chat sort to the top of chat listings.

## Fix

Normalize impossible Apple-epoch conversions to the zero value in `appleNullTime` — the single function every imported timestamp flows through — so the sentinel never enters the archive (and sorts as oldest rather than newest). One-line, root-cause fix; no public JSON model types change.

## Test

`TestAppleNullTimeNormalizesSentinel` covers: a sentinel converts to zero, a valid timestamp still converts correctly, and invalid/non-positive input stays zero (unchanged behaviour). `go test ./...` passes.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: rmorgans
